name: PT Bank Central Asia Tbk
summary: Gold standard of Indonesian banking. CASA 84.2% moat widening, FCF Bank +Rp46.9T, trading at P/E 12.95x — historically cheap on macro fear.
strengths: CASA 84.2% — highest Indonesia, widening 7 years straight — structural cost-of-fund moat.
FCF Bank +Rp46.9T — cash-compounding franchise; FCF yield on equity 16.7%.
ROE 20.43% — best KBMI-4; consistent earnings growth across all economic cycles.
Fee income 31% of net profit — non-interest diversification via 4.97M credit cards.
risks: Revenue growth +4.34% compressed by weak consumer credit; auto loans -13.3%.
CKPN provision doubled to Rp4.0T — macro precaution; not yet a credit quality alarm.
Macro fear (IDR, BBM) may keep price compressed below fair value longer than expected.

name: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia (Persero) Tbk
summary: Fair price, NPL still rising. Wait for credit cycle to peak before entering.
strengths: Unmatched rural reach — 5,086 BRI Units + mobile/ship terraces, 34.5M ultra-micro borrowers.
NIM 7.8% structurally highest in KBMI-4; micro segment captive pricing.
CASA improving 67.3% → 70.6%, structural funding cost advantage.
risks: NPL 3.10% — joint highest in KBMI-4, peak not yet confirmed.
FCF Bank crushed in 2025 (IDR 17T vs IDR 57T net income) due to corporate push.
LDR 94.2% above OJK ideal range, liquidity buffer thin.
Net income declining 5.8% YoY, CKPN +16.9%.

name: PT Bank Mandiri (Persero) Tbk
summary: Best-in-class asset quality, but CASA decline pressuring NIM. Price debatable.
strengths: Best NPL in KBMI-4 (1.19%), corp-comm portfolio quality intact.
CASA still 2nd after BCA; CAR well above regulatory minimum.
Implicit government liquidity backstop via Danantara.
risks: CASA fell 77.7% → 72.8%, CoF rising, NIM compressing.
Deposito growing faster than giro/tabungan — funding mix deteriorating.
State control = political risk + program-mandate lending exposure.

name: PT Bank Negara Indonesia (Persero) Tbk
summary: Lowest NIM among Big-4 (3.8%) due to SOE-corporate tilt. Profit fell 7.2% YoY despite 16% loan growth. Priced at fair value — zero MOS.
strengths: Credit quality healing: NPL 4.3% (2020) → 1.9% (2025); LAR 28.7% → 8.5%.
KBMI-4 systemic bank — implicit state backstop, stable franchise.
CAR 21.8% well-capitalised; fee income +13.4% YoY.
risks: NIM structural compression: 4.81% (2022) → 3.8% (2025) — SOE corp loans yield only 6.4%.
Net profit -7.2% YoY despite 15.9% loan growth — volume can no longer offset margin.
RPT deposits 27.9% of total — concentrated state-entity funding, rotation risk.
MOS essentially zero at IDR 3,770; fair value IDR 3,200–3,400 range.

name: PT Bank OCBC NISP Tbk
summary: Marginal buy / watch. Clean book, structural CASA improvement, P/E 6.6x cheap.
strengths: NPL 2nd best in peer set (only MEGA lower); corp-comm discipline.
CASA 40% → 56.6% over 5yr — depositor trust compounding.
Earnings convert cleanly to owner cash; no manipulation signal.
risks: NIM mid-pack (4.3%) — structurally lower than retail-focused peers.
NPL ticked up 1.8% → 2.0% QoQ, watch for further deterioration.
Smaller scale (KBMI 3) = less moat than BCA/BMRI/BBRI.

name: PT Cisarua Mountain Dairy Tbk
summary: Good business at wrong price. Market paying ~374% premium to conservative fair value.
strengths: Niche yogurt leadership (Cimory); expanding gross margins.
Founder-led (Sutantio family ~75%) + General Atlantic ~5.6% as institutional anchor.
Cash conversion clean; income/cashflow internally consistent.
risks: USD/IDR exposure on imported milk powder & beef — gross margin sensitive.
ICBP-scale entrant could disrupt yogurt moat.
Dairy demand has structural ceiling (Kanzler segment more positive).
Price extreme — 374% premium to fair value.

name: PT Hartadinata Abadi Tbk
summary: Watch list — not buy. Gross margin collapse + heavy debt-funded inventory.
strengths: LBMA certification potential as catalyst.
2025 OCF turnaround after 6 negative years.
Gold inventory build benefits from gold rally tailwind.
risks: Gross margin collapse 9.4% → 4.3% with no floor.
D/A balloon 28.9% → 74.4% — leverage on a declining-margin business.
Inventory = 65.6% of total assets, mostly debt-funded.
Cash-buy-slow-sell quirk amplifies WC drag in gold rallies.

name: PT Bank Danamon Indonesia Tbk
summary: Fair price, FCF Bank negative. ADMF moat intact but CASA declining. Wait for cheaper entry.
strengths: MUFG 92.5% ownership — stable foreign parent, no promoter/governance risk.
ADMF consumer financing moat: ~39% of interest income from only 12% of assets.
NPL 1.7% — best in KBMI-3 class, improving from 1.9% in 2024.
risks: CASA falling structurally (42.3% vs 63.4% peak 2022) → cost of funds rising.
FCF Bank negative 2 years running — all profit absorbed by RWA growth.
LDR 95.5%, above OJK ideal (78–92%) — limited lending room.
ADMF dependency: 32% of loan book, any ADMF deterioration = BDMN deterioration.

name: PT Bank Syariah Indonesia Tbk
summary: Dominant syariah bank (46% market share), FCF Bank positive. Premium valuation at P/BV 1.67x — let it come to you.
strengths: 46.1% Islamic banking market share in financing — structural monopoly position.
FCF Bank positive (+Rp2.65T) — generates real free cash unlike BDMN.
NPF 1.81% improving since 2021 post-merger peak (2.93%).
Gold financing +78% YoY — unique product with Rahn collateral moat.
risks: FaR 9.2% elevated — Rp40.2T Sumatra disaster exposure; watch Q2 2026 data.
Danantara governance: BMRI stake transferred Jan 2026; strategic direction uncertain.
P/BV 1.67x — market already pricing growth; no margin of safety at current price.
G&A high (28.7% of gross revenue) — depreciation from expansion; normalization needed.

name: PP London Sumatera Indonesia Tbk
summary: Qualified buy. Debt-free, healthy tree age, deep discount, P/E ~5x annualised.
strengths: Debt-free balance sheet — survives down-cycles intact.
Tree age 49% in productive 7–20yr band — no replant capex wave imminent.
OER ~26% above sector target band (22–25%).
H1 2024 improving from El Niño 2023 drag; ASP holding.
risks: RPT 80% sales to SIMP — fairness must be checked vs regional reference price.
Cyclical ASP exposure; 2020–2022 peak gross margin 32–33% may not return.
Salim group concentration risk.

name: PT Adira Dinamika Multi Finance Tbk
summary: Largest multifinance by new bookings (IDR 43.2T), backed by MUFG via BDMN. Profitable but NI declining -26.7% CAGR over 7 years. Market cap above fair value — wait for cheaper entry.
strengths: Largest multifinance by new financing (IDR 43.2T FY2025); 877-office network, 66% outside Java.
BDMN/MUFG parent: unlimited joint financing facility + USD 100M MUFG term loan (Nov 2025).
NPF 2.0% improving; NIM 17.3% typical high-margin for consumer finance.
MFIN merger adds scale without integration friction (affiliated absorb).
risks: Net income CAGR -26.7% (2019–2025) — structural earnings compression over 6 years.
Market share declining: motorcycle 11.8%→8.5%, car 4.8%→4.0%.
Automotive market weak: motorcycle +1% YoY, car sales subdued.
MPL mix shift (IDR 13.3T) — highest-yield but highest-risk; NPF risk rising.

name: PT Bank Raya Indonesia Tbk
summary: Digital transformation failed on credit quality. NPL 14.02% (worst among all IDX banking peers), net loss FY2025, negative retained earnings -IDR 3.2T. Revenue -56.6% CAGR over 7 years. Business gate FAIL.
strengths: BRI parent backing (86.85%) — solvency not at risk, CAR 40.25%.
Raya App 977K users — digital acquisition infrastructure exists.
NIM ~10.1% — digital lending spread high (if credit quality were clean).
risks: NPL 14.02% — worst among all 20 IDX banking peers; digital loan credit quality collapsed.
Net loss FY2025 -IDR 498B; FCF Bank negative; two loss years out of 7.
Retained earnings -IDR 3,223B — all equity from BRI capital injections, not earnings.
Revenue -56.6% CAGR 2019–2025 — structural decline, not cyclical.
Value trap: P/BV 1.49x premium on a bank with destroyed fundamentals (BRI rescue narrative priced in).

name: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara Tbk
sectorLabel: Infrastructure — Tower / Telco infra, Jarum Group-controlled
summary: Slow growing but durable archetype. Largest private tower co + biggest fiber footprint, contract-locked recurring revenue. Sitting at upper edge of fair value (~15% overvalued). Wait for IDR ~380 entry.
strengths: Independent ownership (Jarum Group, not telco-affiliated) — best-in-class pricing power; ASP/tower 50% above MTEL.
Largest fiber footprint in Indonesia (258K km vs MTEL 40K) — supports services diversification + contract-bundling moat.
Long-term operator contracts (10+ years) — locked recurring revenue; counterparty default risk low.
FCF strong + growing every year since 2020 — durability proof for the archetype.
Capex declining trajectory — mature-phase optimization signal; less reinvestment burden ahead.
Right-issue 2025 was purposeful (deleveraging) — not chronic dilution.
risks: Tenancy Ratio declining since 2021 peak — tower additions outpacing tenant additions; margin compression structural.
Net margin 39.9% (2021) → 26.4% (FY2024) — D&A + finance cost growing faster than revenue.
Operator merger contract-roll risk: XLSmart (XL+Smartfren) consolidation will compress TR 2027-2030 as contracts roll.
Substitute threat (Starlink + KAI rail-fiber) reduces tower demand at the margin in Java; outer islands insulated.
Hartono allegation (Nov 2025) — open reputation issue; monitor resolution.
High leverage IDR 44.5T interest-bearing debt — tolerable but tight macro (rate hike) would compress further.

name: PT Astra Otoparts Tbk
summary: Component Supplier with JV Cash Engine archetype. Pricing power confirmed (GP 12% → 16% across cycle), flywheel intact, IDR 725B/yr JV dividend stream. Priced near fair value in front of Mar-2026 BYD/Vinfast pabrik shock. Buy trigger ~IDR 2,000 or catalyst clarity.
strengths: Pricing power confirmed — GP margin held/expanded across commodity-cost cycle (Buffett #1 criterion).
JV cash engine — IDR 725B FY2024 dividend received from Denso/GS Battery/Kayaba; ~108% payout per associate footnote sample.
Flywheel intact — brand → 633 outlets distribution → volume → cost-per-unit ↓ → competitive price → market share → brand reinforced.
Astra Group 80% / Jardine ultimate — multi-decade governance continuity; clear chain of control.
OCF positive every year including Covid 2020 (when income was negative) — durability evidence.
Production no.1 in IDX auto-component peer set; deepest brand portfolio (GS Astra, Aspira, Federal, Denso, Kayaba).
Sticky OEM contracts — Astra Honda Motor 22% revenue, Daihatsu 7%; component compatibility = switching cost.
IDR 4.4T cash hoard — defensive war-chest in front of Mar-2026 disruption catalyst.
risks: Vertical-supply-chain disruption — BYD + Vinfast pabrik commissioning Mar-2026 removes AUTO from those volumes; partnership status currently none.
CBU-import trend rising — 70K (pre-Covid) → 135K units/yr (2025); imported volume bypasses local component sourcing.
Manufacturing-segment margin tipis (~5–6%) — low margin of error if production volume drops materially.
Counterfeit (kawai) goods — sustained tempo-headwind on aftermarket revenue growth via tokopedia/shopee channels.
Customer concentration in Astra Group (RPT 34% revenue) — not extractive but exposes to Astra capital allocation decisions.
Cash hoard ambiguity — IDR 4.4T idle could be value-creating war chest OR opportunity-cost drag if deployed poorly.
MOS thin — Kemas's webinar NAV says ~28% undervalue; framework conservative says at fair value. Reconciled ~10–25% MOS not enough to absorb BYD/Vinfast hit.
asOf: Q2 2025 actuals + Q3 2025 forecast + Nov 2025 webinar

name: PT Indofood Sukses Makmur Tbk
summary: Salim conglomerate. Bogasari + ICBP + Pinehill = 30-yr compounded moat. Crisis-proven controller (1997/98 BBCA-vs-INDF triage). ROE 13–15% consistent. Successor untested.
metrics: Sold BBCA, kept INDF — correct 30 yr later
strengths: Multi-decade Salim Group governance; proven 1997/98 crisis triage (kept INDF, sold BBCA — correct call).
Bogasari + Bulog terigu partnership = the cash engine; ICBP + Pinehill global mie instan reach.
Conglomerate naturally hedged across commodity cycle (IndoAgri offsets ICBP input-cost spikes).
ROE 13–15% consistent across 8 years — compounder durability.
risks: Successor (post-Anthoni Salim) crisis-handling untested; first major macro shock under new gen is the open watch-item.
Pinehill execution risk — global MENA/Africa expansion is the largest open operational variable.
Consumer valuation rule: cheap entry rare — wait for crisis dislocation.
FX hedging program disclosure required at next ingest (USD bonds + imported wheat exposure).

name: PT Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur Tbk
summary: Pure-branded consumer layer of Salim's food empire. Indomie #2 globally (Indonesia ~11.6% share). ROE peaked ~20% pre-2022, ~15% currently; commodity-cycle sensitive.
metrics: Input side (wheat); USD bonds at INDF parent level
strengths: Branded consumer dominance in mie instan; #2 global market position.
Down-trading defensive — mie instan is the destination of crisis consumption reallocation, not casualty.
Salim Group governance proven through 1997/98 crisis (parent-level read).
ROE 15–20% high-quality consumer band.
risks: Margin compresses on commodity input cost spikes (wheat, CPO); 2022 cited as proof event.
Consumer valuation premium: cheap entry rare; up to 2x BV normal-condition.
Wings / Mie Sedaap competitive intensity in domestic share defence.
Successor crisis-handling untested (carries through from INDF parent).

name: PT Ultrajaya Milk Industry & Trading Company Tbk
summary: UHT dairy market leader. Q1 2026 revenue +20% / net profit +35%. Net-cash (cash Rp 3.7T, debt < Rp 2B). 100% payout legitimate per Andhika doctrine.
strengths: UHT category leader; net-cash balance sheet; operating leverage active (NP growing ahead of revenue).
Per-capita dairy consumption upside structural (3x vs Malaysia / India peers) — economic, not biological constraint.
100% dividend payout legitimate per Andhika's innovation-runway test (mos_philosophy.md §8.5).
Buffett-aligned business profile: low R&D burden, stable consumer product.
risks: Imported milk powder → USD/IDR input exposure; hedging disclosure required at xlsx.
CMRY competitive position in adjacent dairy categories (yogurt + processed).
Consumer valuation premium: 2x BV normal-condition; cheap entry only at crisis.
Dividend continuity — deviation from 100% payout without articulated runway = flag.

name: PT Gudang Garam Tbk
summary: Tobacco. Andhika acknowledges 3-yr-prior thesis was wrong. Cukai treadmill + illegal-cigarette down trading + Non-Core Capital Trap (bandara/tol). Price Rp 90,000 peak → Rp 8,500 (~91% drawdown).
metrics: FAILED — capital deployed bandara + jalan tol non-core
strengths: FX-insulated — raw materials (tembakau, cengkeh) domestic; no IDR-collapse exposure.
Demand floor structural — addictive product, persistent consumption pattern.
Potential catalyst: Menkeu Purbaya pledged enforcement against illegal cigarettes (force into legal taxable base).
Brand premium retains price-insensitive customer base; survives within retained loyalist segment.
risks: Cukai treadmill (Sri Mulyani era) compressed margin; volume migrated to illegal cigarettes (down trading inside tobacco).
Non-Core Capital Trap: management deploys cash into bandara + jalan tol — fails CEO duty test (Letter #014 verbatim).
Recovery from illegal-cig enforcement is asymmetric — down-traded customers may not return at parity.
New cheap legal substitute (sub-cukai-tier entry) reproduces the illegal-substitute drain through legal channels.
Value-trap risk: cheap multiple does not auto-equal opportunity (Letter #013 rule).
